comments:
WanMove trajectory export format types.

WanMove is a trajectory-based video generation system that uses
point tracks to guide motion in generated videos. This module
provides types for defining flow patterns, morph shapes, and
trajectory data.

## Architecture

This module mirrors `Lattice.Schemas.Exports.WanMoveSchema` from the
Haskell backend, ensuring type-level compatibility for serialization.

## Flow Patterns

- Spiral: Points rotate inward/outward
- Wave: Sinusoidal motion patterns
- Explosion: Radial expansion from center
- Vortex: Rotating swirl effect
- DataRiver: Flow driven by data values
- Morph: Shape-to-shape transformation
- Swarm: Particle-like collective motion

## Constants

- Max dimension: 8192px (width or height)
- Max points: 10000 per trajectory
- Max frames: 10000 per export
- Max FPS: 120

## Architecture Note

This module exceeds the standard 500-line limit due to schema coherence.
All 9 enum types (FlowPattern, MorphShapeType, MorphEasing, ShapeEasing,
AttractorType, DataMapping, ForceFalloff, InitialDistribution, ShapeType)
plus trajectory structures form a single export format that mirrors
`Lattice.Schemas.Exports.WanMoveSchema`. Splitting would fragment a
cohesive API that agents need to use as a unit.

declarations.comments:
Available flow pattern types for trajectory generation.

Each pattern defines how points move over time in the generated video.
declarations.title: FlowPattern

declarations.comments: Convert flow pattern to string identifier.
declarations.title: flowPatternToString

declarations.comments: Morph source and target shape types.
declarations.title: MorphShapeType

declarations.comments: Convert morph shape type to string.
declarations.title: morphShapeTypeToString

declarations.comments: Parse morph shape type from string.
declarations.title: morphShapeTypeFromString

declarations.comments:
Extended easing functions for shape morphing.

Includes elastic and bounce effects not available in basic morph easing.
declarations.title: ShapeEasing

declarations.comments:
Strange attractor types for complex flow patterns.

These are mathematical attractors that produce deterministic but
chaotic-looking motion patterns.
declarations.title: AttractorType

declarations.comments:
Data mapping types for data-driven flows.

Defines how external data values map to motion parameters.
declarations.title: DataMapping

declarations.comments: Falloff types for force points in force field flows.
declarations.title: ForceFalloff

declarations.comments: Initial distribution types for point placement.
declarations.title: InitialDistribution

declarations.comments: Shape types for shape morphing and arrangement.
declarations.title: ShapeType

declarations.comments: Maximum dimension (width or height) in pixels.
declarations.title: wanMoveMaxDimension

declarations.comments: Maximum number of track points per trajectory.
declarations.title: wanMoveMaxPoints

declarations.comments: Maximum number of frames per export.
declarations.title: wanMoveMaxFrames

declarations.comments:
A single track point with 2D coordinates.

Coordinates are normalized to [0, dimension] range.
declarations.title: TrackPoint

declarations.comments: Smart constructor for track point with bounds validation.
declarations.title: mkTrackPoint

declarations.comments:
WanMove trajectory metadata.

Contains dimensions and timing information for the trajectory export.
declarations.title: WanMoveMetadata

declarations.comments: Smart constructor for metadata with validation.
declarations.title: mkWanMoveMetadata

declarations.comments:
Complete WanMove trajectory structure.

Contains track data and visibility flags for each point across frames.
declarations.title: WanMoveTrajectory

declarations.comments: Smart constructor for trajectory with validation.
declarations.title: mkWanMoveTrajectory

declarations.comments: Check if a track point is valid within given dimensions.
declarations.title: isValidTrackPoint

declarations.comments: Map a function over all track points in a trajectory.
declarations.title: mapTrackPoints

declarations.comments: Flip track points vertically (invert Y coordinates).
declarations.title: flipTrajectoryVertical

declarations.comments: Flip track points horizontally (invert X coordinates).
declarations.title: flipTrajectoryHorizontal

declarations.comments: Translate all track points by an offset.
declarations.title: translateTrajectory

declarations.comments:
Calculate squared distance between two track points.

Use this when comparing distances (avoids sqrt). 
For actual distance, use sqrt from Data.Number.
declarations.title: trackPointDistanceSquared

declarations.comments: Get all flow pattern strings for UI dropdowns.
declarations.title: flowPatternStrings

declarations.comments: Get all shape type strings for UI dropdowns.
declarations.title: shapeTypeStrings
